zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Wall於1463年出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾普雷斯頓時，他的父親Evan Wall II50歲，母親Elizabeth Anynson48歲。他於大約1488年與Margaret Whalley在英國英格蘭藍卡爾普雷斯頓結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1531年在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾阿列沃斯享年68歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Wall于1463年出生在英国英格兰藍卡爾普雷斯頓时，他的父亲Evan Wall II50岁，母亲Elizabeth Anynson48岁。他于大约1488年与Margaret Whalley在英国英格兰藍卡爾普雷斯頓结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1531年在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾阿列沃斯享年68岁。

nameMeanings.name: Wall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by a stone-built wall, e.g. one used to fortify a town or to keep back the encroachment of the sea (Middle English wall ‘wall’, Old English wall, weall, from Latin vallum ‘rampart, palisade’), or a habitational name from a place so named, such as Wall (Staffordshire).
English (West Midlands): topographic name for someone who lived by a spring or stream, northern Middle English wall(e) (Old English (Mercian) wæll(a); compare Well ).
Irish (of Norman origin): Anglicized form of de Valle (Gaelicized form de Bhál), the name of a Norman family established in Munster and Connacht.

variants.name: Wallander
meanings: Swedish: variant of Wall , with the addition of the suffix -ander, derived from Greek anēr (genitive andros) ‘man’.

variants.name: Wallenberg
meanings: Swedish: ornamental name composed of the elements Wall + the plural suffix - en (originally German) + berg ‘mountain, hill’.

History: While working as a businessman in Budapest in 1944, Raoul Wallenberg (1912–?47) was entrusted by the Swedish government with the protection of Hungarian Jews from the Nazis. Wallenberg helped about 95,000 Jews to escape death by issuing them Swedish passports. When Soviet forces took control of Budapest in 1945, he was arrested, taken to Moscow, and imprisoned. The Soviet authorities stated that he died in prison in 1947.

variants.name: Well
meanings: English (London): topographic name for someone who lived near a spring or stream, from Middle English well(e) ‘spring, stream, well’ (Old English well(a)).
German: from a short form of the personal names Wallo, Walilo.
German: nickname from Middle High German wël ‘round’.

variants.name: Walle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alberto, Arnulfo, Efrain, Emilia, Evodio, Frederico, Jaime, Javier, Jesus, Jose, Juan, Justo.
English and German: variant of   Wall  . This surname is also found in Mexico.

variants.name: Wallen
meanings: Swedish (mainly Wallén): topographic or ornamental name composed of a variant of the element vall ‘grassy bank, pasture’ (see Wall ) or the root of Latin vallis ‘valley’ (representing Swedish dal) + the adjectival suffix -én/-en, a derivative of Latin -enius ‘relating to’. Compare Wallin and Walen .

variants.name: Walls
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of Wall , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish (Derry): in Ireland this name is also sometimes a variant of   Walsh  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. en.lifeSummary: When Richard Wall was born in 1463, in Preston, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Evan Wall II, was 50 and his mother, Elizabeth Anynson, was 48. He married Margaret Whalley about 1488, in Preston, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. He died in 1531, in Ashleworth, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Wall est né en 1463, à Preston, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Evan Wall II, avait 50 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Anynson, avait 48 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Whalley environ 1488, à Preston, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en 1531, à Ashleworth, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 68 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Wall 1463 in Preston, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Evan Wall II 50 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Anynson 48. Er hat um 1488 in Preston, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom Margaret Whalley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb 1531 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Ashleworth, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Wall è nato il nel 1463 nel Preston, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Evan Wall II, aveva 50 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Anynson, aveva 48 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Whalley circa 1488, nel Preston, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1531, nel Ashleworth, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 68 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Wallが1463年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアプレストンで生まれた時，父Evan Wall IIは50歳，母Elizabeth Anynsonは48歳であった。約1488年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアプレストンでMargaret Whalleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1531年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアアシュルオルスで68歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Wall(이)가 1463년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 프레스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Evan Wall II(은)는 50세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Anynson(은)는 48세였다. 그는 대략 1488년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 프레스턴에서 Margaret Whalley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1531년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 애슐루르스에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Margaret Whalley aproximadamente 1488, em Preston, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1531, em Ashleworth, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 68 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Wall родился в 1463 г. в местности престон, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Evan Wall II было 50 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Anynson, 48. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Whalley около 1488 г. в местности престон, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер в 1531 г. в местности ашльворт, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 68 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Wall nació en 1463, en Preston, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Evan Wall II, tenía 50 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Anynson, tenía 48 años. Se casó con Margaret Whalley aproximadamente 1488, en Preston, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1531, en Ashleworth, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 68 años.
